questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following ions is colourless in solution?
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{V}(23)=[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^{3}, 4 s^{2}$
$\mathrm{V}^{3+}=[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 d^{2}, 4 s^{0} \quad$ (two unpaired electrons)
$$
\begin{array}{l}
\operatorname{Cr}(24)=[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 d^{5}, 4 s^{1} \\
\mathrm{Cr}^{3+}=[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 d^{3}, 4 s^{0}
\end{array}
$$
(three unpaired electrons)
$$
\operatorname{Co}(27)=[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 d^{7}, 4 s^{2}
$$
$$
\mathrm{Co}^{2+}=[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 \mathrm{~d}^{7}, 4 s^{0}
$$
(three unpaired electrons)
$$
\mathrm{Sc}(21)=[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 d^{7}, 4 s^{2}
$$
$\mathrm{Sc}^{3+}=[\mathrm{Ar}] 3 d^{0}, 4 s^{0} \quad$ (no unpaired electrons)
Thus, in $\mathrm{Sc}^{3+}$ no unpaired $d$ -electron is present. Hence, no $d-d$ transition is possible and it is colourless ion.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Most common oxidation states of Ce (cerium) are
questions.solution:
Cerium $\mathrm{Ce}_{58}=[\mathrm{Xe}] 4 f^{1}, 5 d^{1}, 6 s^{2}$
Its most stable oxidation state is $+3$ but $+4$ is also existing.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following statements is not correct?
options: During respiration the size of $\mathrm{Fe}^{2+}$ increases when it changes from diamagnetic to paramagnetic state.
Four heme groups are present in haemoglobin
Heme is the prosthetic group and it is non-protein part.
questions.solution: In oxyhaemoglobin $\mathrm{Fe}^{2+}$ is not paramagnetic.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Ferrous ion charges to $X$ ion, on reacting with acidified hydrogen peroxide.
The number of $d$-electrons present in $X$ and its magnetic moment (in BM) are, respectively :
questions.solution:
Ferrous ion $\left(\mathrm{Fe}^{2+}\right)$ changes to ferric ion $\mathrm{Fe}^{3+}$ on reacting with acidified $\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2$ as 
$2 \mathrm{~K}_4\left[\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{CN})_6\right]+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4+\mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}_2 \longrightarrow$ $2 \mathrm{~K}_3\left[\mathrm{Fe}(\mathrm{CN})_6\right]+\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{SO}_4+2 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}$
Electronic configuration of $\mathrm{Fe}^{3+}=$
$1 s^2, 2 s^2, 2 p^6, 3 s^2, 3 p^6, 3 d^5$
Number of $d$ electrons $=5$
Magnetic moment $=\sqrt{n(n+2)}$
$=\sqrt{5(5+2)}=5.92 \mathrm{BM}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: German silver contains which of the following metals?
questions.solution: German silver (nickel silver) is an alloy of copper, zinc and nickel often in the proportions $5: 2: 2$. It is used in cheap jewellery and cutlery.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: What is the final chemical form of Gold (Au) when it is dissolved in aqua-regia?
questions.solution:
When gold (Au) dissolved in aqua-regia, it forms chloroauric $\left(\mathrm{HAuCl}_4\right)$ ion. It will react very slowly with chlorin gas at room temperature to form gold chloride. We know that aqua-regia is
$$
\mathrm{Au}+\mathrm{HCl}+\mathrm{HNO}_3 \stackrel{3 \mathrm{Cl}+\mathrm{HNO}_3}{\left.\mathrm{HuCl}_4\right]^{-}}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Assertion (A) : Transition elements have higher enthalpies of atomization.
Reason (R) : Large number of unpaired electrons present in transition elements facilitate strong interatomic interaction and strong bonding between atoms.
options: Both (A) and (R) are correct and (R) is the correct explanation of $(A)$
Both (A) and (R) are correct and (R) is not the correct explanation of (A).
questions.solution: More the no. of valence electrons more is the overlapping. This phenomenon also leads to the high melting and boiling point.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following statements is not correct?
options: In the lanthanide series, the ionic radius of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>Ln</mi><mrow><mn>3</mn><mo>+</mo></mrow></msup></math> ions decreases.
<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>La</mi></math> is actually an element of transition series rather than lanthanide series.
Atomic radii of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Zr</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Hf</mi></math> are same because of lanthanide contraction.
questions.solution:
The basic character of lanthanide hydroxides decreases as we go from left to right with increasing atomic number,  i.e., from LaOH3 to LuOH3.

This is due to the lanthanide contraction. Lanthanide contraction is a gradual decrease in the size of lanthanide with the increasing atomic number due to an increase in the nuclear charge. This is caused by the poor shielding of 4f electrons.

In the case of metal hydroxides, greater the size of metal, more is the bond length of M-OH. Hence, it becomes easier to break the bond and releases OH- ions.

In lanthanide, as the size decreases, the basic character also decreases. Hence,  LaOH3 is more basic than LuOH3.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which one of the following lanthanide ions does not exhibit paramagnetism?
questions.solution:
Generally, paramagnetism is shown by lanthanoid ions. The paramagnetism arises due to presence of unpaired electrons in $f$-orbital.
$\mathrm{Lu}^{3+}\left(4 f^{14}\right)$ does not have unpaired electrons.
Hence, it does not exhibit paramagnetism.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Which of the following are correct?
(i) $\mathrm{V}^{2+}$ liberates hydrogen from a dilute acid
(ii) The earlier members of lanthanide series behave more like aluminium
(iii) The 'silver' UK coins are made of $\mathrm{Cu} / \mathrm{Ni}$ alloy
(iv) The maximum oxidation state exhibited by Neptunium is +7
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{V}^{2+}$ is a strong reducing agent and liberates hydrogen from a dilute acid. Thus, (i) is correct.
In chemical behaviour, the earlier members of the lanthanide series behave more like calcium. Thus, (ii) is incorrect.
Silver $\mathrm{UK}$ coins $=\mathrm{Cu}+\mathrm{Ni}$ alloy.
Thus, (iii) is correct.
Neptunium (Np) belongs to the actinides series and has the configuration $5 \mathrm{f}^4 6 \mathrm{~d}^{1 \cdot} 7 \mathrm{~s}^2$ because of which it can show a maximum of +7 oxidation state.
Thus, (iv) is correct.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The auto catalyst in the redox reaction involving acidified potassium permanganate and oxalic acid is
questions.solution:
$$
5 \mathrm{C}_2 \mathrm{O}_4{ }^{2-}+2 \mathrm{MnO}_4{ }^{-}+16 \mathrm{H}^{+} \rightarrow
$$
(Coloured)
$$
\mathrm{Mn}^{2+}+8 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}+10 \mathrm{CO}_2
$$
(Colourless)
Formation of $\mathrm{Mn}^{2+}$ ions, which is indicated by the decrease in the intensity of the colour of the solution, the rate of the reaction increases which was slow initially.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Identify the correct statements from the following.
(i) $\mathrm{Eu}^{2+}$ and $\mathrm{Yb}^{2+}$ are reducing agents.
(ii) The electronic configuration of $\mathrm{Pr}^{3+}$ is $[\mathrm{Xe}] 4 f^3$.
(iii) Aqueous solution of $\mathrm{LaCl}_3$ is colourless.
questions.solution:
(a) $\mathrm{Eu}^{2+}$ and $\mathrm{Yb}^{2+}$ can lose electrons and form $\mathrm{Eu}^{3+}$ and $\mathrm{Yb}^{3+}$.
So, act as reducing agents.
(c) in the aqua solution of $\mathrm{LaCl}_3$ it forms colourless solution.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following is Baeyer's reagent?
questions.solution: Alkaline <math><mi>K</mi><mi>M</mi><mi>n</mi><msub><mrow><mi>O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></msub></math> is Baeyer's reagent.

questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Identify the correct statement
questions.solution:
$\mathrm{Yb}^{2+}=[\mathrm{Xe}] 4 \mathrm{f}^{14}$

Thus, it prefers to lose one electron as shielding is strong and it can form $\mathrm{Yb}^{3+}$ so it acts as a reductant.
$\mathrm{Lu}^{3+}=[\mathrm{Xe}] 4 \mathrm{f}^{14}$ so it is diamagnetic.
$\mathrm{CrO}$ is basic in its low oxidation state of +2 and prefers to attain a state of +6 .
Brass $=\mathrm{Cu}+\mathrm{Zn}$ and $\mathrm{Bronze}=\mathrm{Cu}+\mathrm{Sn}$

questions.q: Identify the correct decreasing order of densities of d -block elements.
questions.solution:
Across the $3 \mathrm{~d}-$ series, density of the elements increases.
$\therefore \mathrm{Ni}>\mathrm{Fe}>\mathrm{Cr}>\mathrm{V}$

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Lanthanoid contraction is due to:
options: Negligible screening effect of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>4</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">f</mi></math> orbitals.
Negligible screening effect of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">f</mi></math> orbital.
questions.solution:
Lanthanide contraction :

  There is a decrement in the size of the atoms and ions of the f- block elements with increasing atomic number from  La  having atomic number 57 through Lu  having atomic number 71. Weak screening effect of the 4f- electrons called Lanthanide contraction
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In context of the lanthanoids, which of the following statement is not correct?
options: There is a gradual decrease in the radii of the members with increasing atomic number in the series.
All the members exhibit +3 oxidation state.
Because of similar properties the separation of lanthanoids is not easy.
Availability of 4f electrons results in the formation of compounds in +4 state for all the members of the series.
questions.solution: Availability of 4f electrons does not result  in the formation of compounds in +4 state for all the members of the series.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: When the ion <math> <mrow> <msub> <mtext>Cr</mtext> <mn>2</mn> </msub> <msubsup> <mtext>O</mtext> <mn>7</mn> <mrow> <mn>2</mn> <mo>-</mo> </mrow> </msubsup> </mrow> </math> acts as an oxidant in acidic aqueous solution the ion <math> <mrow> <msup> <mtext>Cr</mtext> <mrow> <mn>3</mn> <mo>+</mo> </mrow> </msup> </mrow> </math> is formed. How many moles of Sn<sup>2</sup><sup>+</sup> would be oxidised to Sn<sup>4</sup><sup>+</sup> by one mole of <math> <mrow> <msub> <mtext>Cr</mtext> <mn>2</mn> </msub> <msubsup> <mtext>O</mtext> <mn>7</mn> <mrow> <mn>2</mn> <mo>-</mo> </mrow> </msubsup> </mrow> </math> ions?
questions.solution: <math><mrow>  <mfenced open="[" close="]" separators=",">    <mrow>      <msubsup>              <mtext>Cr</mtext>            <mn>2</mn>          <mrow>            <mn>6</mn>            <mo>+</mo>          </mrow>      </msubsup>      <mo>+</mo>            <mn>6</mn>      <mtext>e</mtext>      <mo>→</mo>      <mn>2</mn>      <msup>              <mtext>Cr</mtext>          <mrow>            <mn>3</mn>            <mo>+</mo>          </mrow>      </msup>    </mrow>  </mfenced>  <mo>×</mo>  <mn>1</mn>  <mtext>;  </mtext>  <mfenced open="[" close="]" separators=",">    <mrow>      <msup>              <mtext>Sn</mtext>          <mrow>            <mn>2</mn>            <mo>+</mo>          </mrow>      </msup>      <mo>→</mo>      <msup>              <mtext>Sn</mtext>          <mrow>            <mn>4</mn>            <mo>+</mo>          </mrow>      </msup>      <mo>+</mo>      <mn>2</mn>      <mtext>e</mtext>    </mrow>  </mfenced>  <mo>×</mo>  <mn>3</mn></mrow></math>

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Statement (I) $\mathrm{Co}^{2+}$ has higher magnetic moment than $\mathrm{Cr}^{3+}$.
Statement (II) Ionisation enthalpies of Ce, Pr and $\mathrm{Nd}$ are higher than $\mathrm{Th}, \mathrm{Pa}, \mathrm{U}$.
Which of the following is correct?
options: (l) is correct but (II) is not correct
(l) is not correct but (II) is correct
questions.solution: Statement (I) is not correct, as magnetic moment of $\mathrm{Cr}^{3+}$ is more than of $\mathrm{CO}^{2+}$. But, statement (II) is correct, as I.P. of Ce, Pr and Nd are higher than th, Pa and U. Thus, the correct option is (4).
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following set of elements do not possess $f$-electrons?
questions.solution:
Electronic configurations of
(i) Lanthanum (La) with $(Z=57)$ is
$$
[\mathrm{Xe}]_{54} \cdot 5 d^1 \cdot 6 s^2
$$
(ii) Uranium (U) with $(Z=92)$ is
$$
[\mathrm{Rn}]_{86} \cdot 5 f^3 \cdot 6 d^1 \cdot 7 s^2
$$
(iii) Lawrencium ( $\operatorname{Lr})$ with $(Z=103)$ is
$$
[\mathrm{Rn}]_{86} \cdot 5 f^{14} \cdot 6 d^1 \cdot 7 s^2
$$

(iv)Thorium (Th) with $(Z=90)$ is
$$
[\mathrm{Rn}]_{86} \cdot 5 f^1 \cdot 6 d^1 \cdot 7 s^2 \equiv[\mathrm{Rn}]_{86} 6 d^2 \cdot 7 s^2
$$
(due to nearly same energy of $5 f$ and $6 d$-orbitals).
(v) Actinium (Ac) with $(Z=89)$ is
$$
[\mathrm{Rn}]_{86} \cdot 6 d^1 \cdot 7 s^2
$$
(VI) Cerium (Ce) with $(Z=58)$ is
$$
[\mathrm{Xe}]_{54} \cdot 4 f^1 \cdot 5 d^1 \cdot 6 s^2
$$

On the basis of above given electronic configuration, elements La, Ac, and Th do not possess $f$-electrons.

Hence, option (c) is the correct answer.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: When zinc is reacted with dilute and concentrated nitric acid, respectively, gives rise to:
options: <math><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></math>

<math><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></math>

<math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math>

<math><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">d</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">N</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></math>
questions.solution:
Zn on reaction with HNO3
Zinc reacts with the dilute nitric acid to form the nitrous oxide gas. It is also known as the laughing gas.

4Zn+10HNO3dil→4ZnNO32+N2O+5H2O 

Zinc reacts with the concentrated nitric acid to form the brown colour nitrogen dioxide gas.
Zn+4HNO3conc.→ZnNO32+2NO2+2H2O
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following can produce a colour change from yellowish brown to light green in acidified aqueous solution of Iron (III) chloride?
questions.solution:
Zn+2HCl→ZnCl2+2(H)
FeCl3+(H)→FeCl2+HCl
Zinc and HCl produce nascent hydrogen which reduces yellowish brown FeCl3 to light green FeCl2 .
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Identify the correct statements from the following :
I. The ionic radius of \(\mathrm{Pr}^{3+}, \mathrm{Dy}^{3+}\) and \(\mathrm{Sm}^{3+}\) follow the order, \(\mathrm{Sm}^{3+} > \mathrm{Pr}^{3+} > \mathrm{Dy}^{3+}\).
II. \(\mathrm{Eu}^{2+}\) acts as strong reducing reagent.
III. Pu exhibits +7 oxidation state.
questions.solution:
(i) The ionic radius of lanthanoids follow the following relation:
Ionic radius \(\propto \frac{1}{\text { atomic number }}\)
Atomic number \((Z)\) for \(\mathrm{Pr}=59\)
Atomic number \((Z)\) for \(\mathrm{Sm}=62\)
Atomic number \((Z)\) for \(D y=66\)
and has ionic radii for positive ions as given below:
\(\begin{aligned}
\mathrm{Pr}^{3+} & =1.013 Å \\
\mathrm{Sm}^{3+} & =0.964 Å \\
\mathrm{Dy}^{3+} & =0.908 Å
\end{aligned}\)
Thus, statement (i) is not correct.
(ii) Element Eu \((Z=63)\) can exist in \((+) 2\) and \((+) 3\) oxidation state.
\(\therefore(+)\) 3oxidation state is the most common oxidation state for lanthanoids.
Thus, (+) 2oxidation state oxides oxidises to (+) 3 easily and \(\mathrm{Eu}^{2+}\) can act as strong reducing agent.
Hence, the statement (II) is correct.
(iii) The electronic configuation for
\(\text {(Pu), }(Z=94)=5 f^6 6 d^0 7 s^2\)
Therefore, it can use maximum of \(6+2=8\) electrons.
Thus, can show +7 oxidation state.
Thus, statement (III) is also correct.
Hence, option (d) is the correct answer.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The number f-electrons in +3 oxidation state of gadolinium $(Z=64)$ is $x$ and in +2 oxidation state of Ytterbium $(\mathrm{Z}=70)$ is $\mathrm{y}$. The sum of $\mathrm{x}$ and $\mathrm{y}$ is
questions.solution:
The outer electronic configuration for $\mathrm{Gd}$ and $\mathrm{Yb}$ are:-
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{Gd}=4 f^7 5 \mathrm{~d}^1 6 \mathrm{~s}^2 \\
& \mathrm{Yb}=4 f^{14} 6 \mathrm{~s}^2
\end{aligned}$
Thus, $\mathrm{Gd}^{3+}=4 f^7$ and $\mathrm{yb}^{2+}=4 f^{14}$
Thus, $x=7$ and $y=14$
$\Rightarrow x+y=7+14=21$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Copper sulphate is prepared by blowing a current of air through Cu scrap and dilute H<sub>2</sub>SO<sub>4</sub>. Dilute HNO<sub>3</sub> is also added
options: Which combines with H<sub>2</sub>SO<sub>4</sub> to give a very strong oxidising mixture and oxidises Cu to Cu<sup>2+</sup>
To oxidise Cu to Cu<sup>2+</sup> which then from CuSO<sub>4</sub> with dilute H<sub>2</sub>SO<sub>4</sub>
To increase ionisation of H<sub>2</sub>SO<sub>4</sub> to give <math> <mrow> <msubsup> <mtext>SO</mtext> <mn>4</mn> <mrow> <mn>2</mn> <mo>-</mo> </mrow> </msubsup> </mrow> </math> ions
To oxidise Fe<sup>2+</sup> to Fe<sup>3+</sup> sulphate, which remains in solution after crystallisation of CuSO<sub>4</sub>
questions.solution: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mtable><mtr><mtd><msub><mtext>FeSO</mtext><mn>4</mn></msub></mtd><mtd><munderover><mo>→</mo><msub><mtext>HNO</mtext><mn>3</mn></msub><mtext>Dilute</mtext></munderover></mtd><mtd><msub><mtext>Fe</mtext><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mfenced><msub><mtext>SO</mtext><mn>4</mn></msub></mfenced><mn>3</mn></msub><mo>↓</mo></mtd><mtd><mo>+</mo></mtd><mtd><msub><mtext>CuSO</mtext><mn>4</mn></msub></mtd></mtr><mtr><mtd/><mtd/><mtd><mtext>Remains in solution</mtext></mtd><mtd/><mtd><mtext>Crystallises out</mtext></mtd></mtr></mtable></math>
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which following pair of elements does NOT represent chemical twins?
questions.solution: Chemical twins are those in which the properties of some elements are same. Zr-Rf is not a chemical twin.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The correct increasing order of basic character of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Ce</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>OH</mi><msub><mo>)</mo><mn>3</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mi>Gd</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>OH</mi><msub><mo>)</mo><mn>3</mn></msub></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Nd</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>OH</mi><msub><mo>)</mo><mn>3</mn></msub></math> is
questions.solution:
Due to lanthanide contraction, as the size of lanthanide ions decreases from left to right La3+to Lu3+, the covalent character of the hydroxides increases and hence, the basic strength decreases. 

So, increasing order of basic characters: Gd(OH)3 <Nd(OH)3 <Ce(OH)3
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In chemical reaction of potassium ferrocyanide and hydrogen peroxide in the presence of dil. <math><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><msub><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></msub></math>
options: Iron does not change its oxidation state
questions.solution:
In reaction of potassium ferrocyanide and hydrogen peroxide in the presence of dil. H2SO4
[Fe(CN)6]4- is oxidised by H2O2 into [Fe(CN)6]3- ions.
2 K 4 [Fe (CN) 6 ]+ H 2 O 2 →2 K 3 [Fe (CN) 6 ]+2KOH
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
In the following reactions, identify $P, Q$ and $R$, respectively
$\text { I. } 3 \mathrm{Fe}_2 \mathrm{O}_3+\mathrm{CO} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{P}+\mathrm{CO}_2 \uparrow$
II. $\mathrm{Fe}_3 \mathrm{O}_4+4 \mathrm{CO} \longrightarrow 3 Q+4 \mathrm{CO}_2 \uparrow$
III. $\mathrm{Fe}_2 \mathrm{O}_3+\mathrm{CO} \longrightarrow 2 R+\mathrm{CO}_2 \uparrow$
questions.solution:
Among the given equations, option (c) will give the required answer, i.e.,
$(P)=\mathrm{Fe}_3 \mathrm{O}_4,(Q)=\mathrm{Fe},(R)=\mathrm{FeO} \text {, i.e. }$
I. $3 \mathrm{Fe}_2 \mathrm{O}_3+\mathrm{CO} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{Fe}_3 \mathrm{O}_4+\mathrm{CO}_2$
II. $\mathrm{Fe}_3 \mathrm{O}_4+4 \mathrm{CO} \longrightarrow 3 \mathrm{Fe}+4 \mathrm{CO}_2$
III. $\mathrm{Fe}_2 \mathrm{O}_3+\mathrm{CO} \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{FeO}+\mathrm{CO}_2$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The yellow colour of chromate ion and orange colour of dichromate ion is due to
questions.solution:
In both of chromate \(\left(\mathrm{CrO}_4^{2-}\right)\) and dichromate \(\left(\mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7^{2-}\right)\) ions, \(\mathrm{Cr}\) is present in +6 oxidation state, i.e. \(d^0\)-configuration. So, \(d-d\) transition is absent in both of the ions.
Their intense colour (yellow of \(\mathrm{CrO}_4^{2-}\) and orange of \(\mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7^{2-}\)) is the charge transfer (ligand \(\longrightarrow\) metal) of oxygen get transfered into vacant \(d\)-orbital of \(\mathrm{Cr}\) (VI).
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which among the following lanthanoids has smallest atomic size?
questions.solution:
Atomic size decreases across the series from left to right.
$\mathrm{Ce}>\mathrm{Pr}>\mathrm{Pm}>\mathrm{Sm}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Identify all the correct statements for Lanthanide contraction.
(A) The covalent properties of the Lanthanide metal hydroxides increases from $\mathrm{La}$ to $\mathrm{Lu}$.
(B) The chemical reactivity decreases from $\mathrm{La}$ to $\mathrm{Lu}$.
(C) $\mathrm{La}(\mathrm{OH})_3$ is more basic than $\mathrm{Lu}(\mathrm{OH})_3$
(D) Zr and Hf have about the same radius.
(E) Separation of Lanthanides from one another is easy
questions.solution:
The change in ionic radii is Lanthanide series is very small, their chemical properties are small. Thus, the separation of Lanthanide is difficult due to consequence of Lanthanide contraction.
The ionic radii of same positive charge of $\mathrm{La}$ to $\mathrm{Lu}$ decreases. So, the polarizing power towards hydroxyl group increases. Therefore the covalent character increases. Thus, $\mathrm{La}(\mathrm{OH})_3$ is more basic than $\mathrm{Lu}(\mathrm{OH})_3$. $\mathrm{Zr}$ and $\mathrm{Hf}$ have the almost same radius as the electrons in the elements after $\mathrm{Zr}$ are added into the deep seated $4 \mathrm{f}$ orbitals which do not take part in the chemical reaction.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Potassium permanganate acts as an oxidant in neutral, alkaline as well as acidic media. The final products obtained from it in the three conditions are, respectively
questions.solution:
In neutral and alkaline medium
MnO4-+2H2O+3e-→MnO2+4OH-
In acidic medium
MnO4-+8H++5e-→Mn2++4H2O
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which among the following elements is a soft element as compared to others
questions.solution:
All transition elements (with the exception of $\mathrm{Zn}, \mathrm{Cd}$ and $\mathrm{Hg}$ ) are very hard.
$\therefore$ Here, $\mathrm{Zn}$ is a soft element as compared to others.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Choose the correct option regarding the following.
Assertion Actinides exhibit a larger number of oxidation states than lanthanides.
Reason There is a large energy gap between $5 f, 6 d$ and $7 s$ sub-shells.
options: Assertion and Reason are correct, and Reason is the correct explanation for the Assertion.
Assertion and Reason are correct, and Reason is not the correct explanation for the Assertion.
Assertion is correct, Reason is wrong.
Assertion is incorrect, Reason is correct.
questions.solution: Actinides exhibit larger oxidation states than lanthanides because of the very small energy gap between $5 f, 6 d$ and $7 s$ subshells. Thus, the outermost electrons get easily excited to the higher energy levels, giving variable oxidation states. Hence, Assertion is correct, Reason is incorrect. Thus, the correct option is (3).
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which of the following statements is TRUE?
options: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>La</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>OH</mi><msub><mo>)</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">3</mi></msub></math> is the least basic among hydroxides of lanthanides.

The oxide <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>Ln</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">2</mi></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">O</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">3</mi></msub></math> reacts with water to form soluble hydroxides.
questions.solution: Zr and Hf both belongs to d-block elements. In d-block elements, the last electron enters into the d-block and due to the poor shielding of f-block elements contraction in the atomic radii as well as ionic radii is observed, therefore both Zr4+ and Hf4+ possess almost the same ionic radii.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Which one of the following statements related to lanthanons is incorrect?
options: The basicity decreases as the ionic radius decreases from <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Pr</mi></math> to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Lu</mi></math>.
All the lanthanons are much more reactive than aluminium.
<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>Ce</mi><mfenced><mrow><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> solutions are widely used as oxidizing agent in volumetric analysis.
questions.solution: All the lanthanons are not much more reactive than aluminium.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Chemistry
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Identify the oxidation states of titanium <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>Z</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>22</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and copper <math><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>Z</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>29</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> in their colourless compounds.
questions.solution:
Ti:[ Ar ]4 s 2 3 d 2        Cu:[ Ar ]4 s 1 3 d 10
T i +4 :[ Ar ]4 s 0 3 d 0    C u +1 :[ Ar ]4 s 0 3 d 10
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
